claim: Ghent to Luxembourg by train requires one change (typically Brussels) with fastest journey ~3h50, average ~4h43, no direct service.
quote: The fastest journey time by train from Ghent to Luxembourg is 3 hours and 50 minutes... There are no direct train services from Ghent to Luxembourg. You'll need to make 1 change along the way.

claim: The Chemin de la Corniche is dubbed 'the most beautiful balcony of Europe' by Luxembourg writer Batty Weber, running along 17th-century Spanish/French ramparts above the Alzette valley.
quote: called by the Luxembourg writer Batty Weber 'the most beautiful balcony of Europe', the path runs along the Alzette valley on the ramparts built by the Spaniards and the French in the 17th century

claim: Luxembourg's free nationwide public transport (since 2020) remains in force in 2026, with mobility budget growing to €3.5 billion.
quote: In 2026, Luxembourg's mobility budget has ballooned to a staggering €3.5 billion, marking an 8% increase year-over-year

claim: Luxembourg ranks among the world's top destinations for Michelin-starred restaurants per capita, with roughly 11-13 starred venues for ~650k inhabitants.
quote: Luxembourg Ranks Second Globally for Michelin-Starred Restaurants per Capita

claim: Vianden Castle (medieval fortress with Romanesque/Gothic halls above the Our river) and Echternach (oldest town in Luxembourg, founded 698 by Willibrord, with abbey and basilica) form the canonical Luxembourg countryside day trip.
quote: explore Vianden Castle's impressive halls and panoramic terraces, before continuing to Echternach, where you'll discover the abbey grounds and charming old town

claim: There is NO Bruges Triennial in 2026 — the next edition runs 24 April – 5 September 2027, after the 2024 edition.
quote: Bruges Triennial 2027 will take place from April 24 – September 5, 2027

claim: TEFAF Maastricht 2026 runs 14-19 March (preview days 12-13 March) at MECC Maastricht — the world's premier fine art and antiques fair.
quote: TEFAF | March 14–19, 2026 | MECC Maastricht

claim: Cologne Karneval 2026 runs 12-17 February: Weiberfastnacht Thu 12 Feb (kicks off 11:11), Rosenmontag parade Mon 16 Feb, Veilchendienstag Tue 17 Feb.
quote: Weiberfastnacht (Women's Carnival): Thursday, 12 February 2026 (from 11:11)... Rosenmontag (Rose Monday): February 16, 2026... Veilchendienstag (Violet Tuesday): 17 February

claim: Strasbourg Christkindelsmärik 2026 (Capitale de Noël) runs late November through 27 December across 9 markets in the historic centre, drawing >2 million visitors.
quote: Strasbourg Christmas markets 2026 schedule begins on November 27... most market stalls remaining open daily until December 27... over 2 million visitors

claim: Keukenhof 2026 opens 19 March – 10 May; peak tulip window 13-25 April; Bloemencorso flower parade Saturday 18 April (busiest day); time-slot booking mandatory.
quote: Keukenhof 2026 runs from 19 March to 10 May... Visit between 9 April and 3 May for peak tulip display... The Bloemencorso flower parade takes place on Saturday 18 April 2026

claim: Tour de France 2026 (4-26 July) starts in Barcelona and stays primarily in Spain/France with Pyrenees and Alps stages — no Belgian or Dutch stages, finish on Champs-Élysées with a Montmartre passage.
quote: race will take place from 4 to 26 July 2026, starting in Barcelona... three stages in northern Spain... entering France and the Pyrenees as early as stage 3

claim: Holland Festival 2026 (79th edition) runs 3-28 June across Amsterdam, spanning music, theatre, dance, visual arts and film.
quote: The 79th edition of the Holland Festival takes place from June 3 to 28, 2026, in Amsterdam

claim: Lille3000 'Saison Méditerranée' runs 15 May – 27 September 2026 with city-wide exhibitions and street art transformations.
quote: Lille3000 runs from May 15 to September 27, 2026, as part of Saison Méditerranée 2026

claim: Brussels Iris Festival 2026 — the official Brussels-Capital Region birthday celebration — falls on 9-10 May with free concerts and street programming.
quote: On May 9 and 10, 2026, celebrate the Brussels-Capital Region's 37th birthday at the Iris Festival

claim: Berlin Festival of Lights 2026 (22nd edition, theme 'Colours of Love') runs 9-18 October, illuminations nightly 7-11pm, free to attend.
quote: Festival of Lights 2026 will take place from October 9th to 18th in Berlin... motto 'Colours of Love'

claim: Aachen Christmas Market 2026 runs 20 November – 23 December around the Cathedral and Town Hall, daily 11am-9pm.
quote: The Aachen Christmas Market will take place from November 20 to December 23, 2026

claim: Cologne Christmas markets 2026 cluster runs roughly 16 November – 23 December, with the iconic Cathedral market 25 Nov – 23 Dec and Heinzels Wintermärchen 16 Nov – 23 Dec.
quote: Christmas market in Cologne will start on 16 November and continue till 23 December 2026... Cologne Cathedral Christmas Market: Nov 25 - Dec 23, 2026

claim: Tomorrowland 2026 returns to De Schorre, Boom across two weekends — 17-19 July and 24-26 July — with the theme 'Consciencia'.
quote: the event will run over two weekends at its spiritual home of Boom, from 17th to 19th and 24th to 26th July, 2026

claim: Belgian school holidays 2026: Carnival break 16-22 February (overlaps Cologne Karneval), Easter/Spring break 6-19 April, Christmas 21 Dec – 3 Jan 2027 — these windows spike train fares and crowd levels.
quote: Carnival break: Monday 16 February to Sunday 22 February 2026; Easter break: Monday 6 April to Sunday 19 April 2026; Christmas break: Monday 21 December 2026 to Sunday 3 January 2027

claim: Magisch Maastricht takes over the Vrijthof in late November through late December annually with a 45m Ferris wheel, ice rink, curling rink and carousel — train-reachable from Ghent via Brussels in ~3h.
quote: Magisch Maastricht Vrijthof takes place at the Vrijthof in Maastricht, and the market includes a skating rink, a curling rink, a carousel, and a 45-meter-high Ferris wheel

claim: Bock Casemates is a 23km network of underground tunnels (originally built 1644) honeycombing the Luxembourg City cliff — UNESCO-listed and the city's signature sight.
quote: underground tunnels that run for 23km underneath Luxembourg City, with the original tunnels being built in 1644

claim: Mudam (Musée d'Art Moderne Grand-Duc Jean) opened 2006, designed by Pritzker-winning architect I.M. Pei on the site of the old Fort Thüngen — Luxembourg's flagship contemporary art museum.
quote: purpose-built building designed by the renowned Sino-American architect Ieoh Ming Pei... The museum stands on the site of the old Fort Thüngen, on the southwestern edge of the Kirchberg-plateau
